original_hypothesis.title: Zero Address Validation Bypass in GaugeV2.setInternalBribe
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: A tautological validation check allows the internal bribe address to be set to the zero address, breaking fee distribution.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The validation `require(_int >= address(0), "ZA");` is always true. This allows an admin to accidentally set `internal_bribe` to `address(0)`. Subsequent calls to `claimFees` will attempt to approve and notify the zero address, causing the transaction to revert and locking LP fees.
deployment_assumptions: Admin misconfigures the internal bribe address
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script evidence of this specific misconfiguration, but the code allows it.

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Tautological require statement provides no actual validation

operational_questions: Is there off-chain validation before admin transactions are signed?
How quickly can the admin correct this if it happens?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin accidentally sets internal_bribe to address(0)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Transaction reverts when calling notifyRewardAmount on address(0)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Fees are locked until admin fixes the address

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low - admin can set a valid address

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
